CHiPS (2017)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $18,600,152Details
International Box Office $8,200,000
Worldwide Box Office $26,800,152
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $6,195,474
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $2,333,780
Total $8,529,254
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Jon Baker and Frank “Ponch” Poncherello have just joined the California Highway Patrol in Los Angeles but for very different reasons. Baker is a beaten up pro motorbiker trying to put his life and marriage back together. Poncherello is a cocky undercover Federal agent investigating a multi-million dollar heist that may be an inside job—inside the CHP. The inexperienced rookie and the hardened pro are teamed together, but clash more than click, so kickstarting a partnership is easier said than done. But with Baker’s bike skills combined with Ponch’s street savvy it might just work…if they don't drive each other crazy along the way.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$7,722,802 (41.5% of total gross)
Legs:2.41 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:69.4% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$25,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.1 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,464 opening theaters/2,464 max. theaters, 3.0 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $24,800,208

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: March 24th, 2017 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
International Releases: March 23rd, 2017 (Wide) (Netherlands)
March 24th, 2017 (Wide), released as CHiPs: Law and Disorder (United Kingdom)
March 24th, 2017 (Wide), released as CHiPs: Магистрални ченгета (Bulgaria)
March 24th, 2017 (Wide), released as Patruliai (Lithuania)
March 24th, 2017 (Wide), released as Patrulha de Doidos (Portugal)
Video Release: June 6th, 2017 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: R for crude sexual content, graphic nudity, pervasive language, some violence and drug use.
(Rating bulletin 2435 (Cert #50580), 7/27/2016)
Running Time: 100 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Directing Yourself, Family Affair, Screenplay Written By the Star
Geographical setting: California, Hollywood
Plot point: Buddy Cop, Corrupt Cops, FBI, Undercover
Source material: Adapted as a Comedy
Subgenre: Action Comedy
Source:Based on TV
Genre:Action
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Warner Bros., Panay Films, RatPac Entertainment, Dune Entertainment
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Jon Baker and Frank “Ponch” Poncherello have just joined the California Highway Patrol in Los Angeles but for very different reasons. Baker is a beaten up pro motorbiker trying to put his life and marriage back together. Poncherello is a cocky undercover Federal agent investigating a multi-million dollar heist that may be an inside job—inside the CHP. The inexperienced rookie and the hardened pro are teamed together, but clash more than click, so kickstarting a partnership is easier said than done. But with Baker’s bike skills combined with Ponch’s street savvy it might just work…if they don't drive each other crazy along the way.
